notes: Synthetic, non-PHI denial-management incident cases for local observability and evaluation workflow validation.

case_payload.input_text: Synthetic denial packet with sparse malformed-style text: reason missing, documentation missing, service context unclear.

incidents.expected_behavior: Runtime completes without mutation and exposes denial intake fields plus node diagnostics.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Malformed denial text may produce unclear denial classification.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect denial_reason, denial_category, routing_action, and node_diagnostics.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied for unclear and conflicting appeal rationale.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic service context has inconsistent necessity language.

incidents.expected_behavior: Ambiguity routes to ESCALATE with AMBIGUOUS governance posture.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Ambiguity could collapse into appeal or write-off certainty.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect ambiguity documentation gap, routing action, and governance posture.

case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic service appears supported by assessment and plan.
case_payload.documentation_summary: Missing note was omitted but can provide and attach for resubmission.

incidents.expected_behavior: Recoverable documentation gap avoids premature WRITE_OFF.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Recoverable evidence could be incorrectly routed to write-off.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied because same-day distinct service modifier was omitted.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic separate service rationale is supported.
case_payload.documentation_summary: Modifier documentation was missing but available for corrected resubmission.

incidents.expected_behavior: Routing action remains inside the bounded denial vocabulary and recoverability is visible.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: A routing action outside the bounded vocabulary would indicate instability.

case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic local-only case with no external service dependency.

incidents.expected_behavior: Local denial scaffold reports completed status and explicit fallback/degraded flags.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Fallback or degraded flags could be absent from local output.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect status, fallback_used, degraded_mode, and node diagnostics.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied with conflicting payer rationale and inconsistent documentation interpretation.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic clinical summary supports service in one section and contradicts support in another.

incidents.expected_behavior: Conflicting evidence remains visible and routes to ESCALATE.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Conflicting evidence could be treated as supported certainty.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect documentation_gaps.ambiguity, routing action, and governance posture.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied despite conflicting specialist and utilization review evidence.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic clinical summary supports service while utilization review disputes necessity.
case_payload.utilization_review_notes: Utilization review says criteria not met.

incidents.expected_behavior: Conflicting evidence routes to ESCALATE with high-risk or ambiguous posture.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Conflict could collapse into unsupported certainty.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied for weak support and missing required criteria.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic packet has weak but non-zero support.
case_payload.medical_necessity_rationale: Weak but non-zero rationale is present.

incidents.expected_failure_mode: Weak evidence could be over-routed to APPEAL certainty.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect routing action and LOW_EVIDENCE or AMBIGUOUS posture.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied for prior authorization mismatch with inconsistent timing.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic service may be supported but authorization timing is unclear.
case_payload.timeline_flags: Authorization appears after service in one note and before service in another.

incidents.expected_failure_mode: Timeline ambiguity could oscillate between RESUBMIT and APPEAL.

case_payload.documentation_summary: Longitudinal evidence is referenced but omitted.
case_payload.lab_summary: Current lab is present but trend is not included.

incidents.expected_behavior: Missing required evidence is extracted and routes to RESUBMIT.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Structured evidence fields could be ignored.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied because modifier support is partial.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic same-day service may be distinct.
case_payload.documentation_summary: Site separation is documented but time separation is missing.
case_payload.coding_specificity_flags: Modifier support is partial and borderline.

incidents.expected_failure_mode: Partial support could be treated as low recoverability.

case_payload.payer_reason: Denied for sparse evidence on specialist-level service.
case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic high-risk context has limited documentation.
case_payload.documentation_summary: Specialist review is needed before final routing certainty.

incidents.expected_failure_mode: Specialist case could be routed to WRITE_OFF without review.

case_payload.clinical_summary: Synthetic case supports local denial graph execution.
case_payload.documentation_summary: Documentation gap is recoverable through corrected packet assembly.

incidents.expected_behavior: Layer 1 alerting reports a latency_spike without changing routing semantics.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Latency anomaly could be hidden from operational metadata.
incidents.evaluation_notes: Inspect alerts, max_alert_severity, status, and error fields.

incidents.expected_behavior: Layer 1 alerting reports missing_required_metadata without changing route or posture.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Missing operational metadata could be treated as governance disagreement.

case_payload.documentation_summary: Packet assembly is malformed but denial fields remain local and synthetic.

incidents.expected_behavior: Layer 1 alerting reports malformed_payload and trace_incomplete without changing governance semantics.
incidents.expected_failure_mode: Malformed operational payload could be hidden in trace details.
